metadata.title: Guideline on the clinical evaluation of direct acting antiviral agents intended for treatment of chronic hepatitis C

rules.rule_text: HCV RNA levels must be determined with a standardised, CE-marked quantitative assay based on real-time PCR technology with a lower limit of detection in the order of 10-15 IU/ml. The same assay should be used for all samples from a single study and whenever possible throughout the clinical development programme.
rules.verbatim_quote: HCV RNA levels must be determined with a standardised, CE-marked quantitative assay based on real-time PCR technology, with a lower limit of detection in the order of 10-15 IU/ml... The same assay should be used for all samples from a single study and, whenever possible, throughout the clinical development programme.

rules.rule_text: HCV genotype determination must use direct sequence analysis with either commercial or validated in-house techniques. The applicant must justify that a sufficiently large portion of the NS5B gene is sequenced, followed by phylogenetic analyses.
rules.verbatim_quote: The method that should be systematically used for genotype determination is direct sequence analysis with either commercial or validated in-house techniques. The applicant should justify that a sufficiently large portion of the NS5B gene is sequenced. Sequence determination should be followed by phylogenetic analyses.

rules.rule_text: The recommended primary endpoint for confirmatory studies is sustained virological response (SVR), defined as undetectable HCV RNA 6 months after completion of therapy (SVR24). Missing SVR24 data in the primary analysis should be considered as non-response.
rules.verbatim_quote: The recommended primary endpoint for confirmatory studies is sustained virological response (SVR), defined as undetectable HCV RNA 6 months after completion of therapy, regardless of the scheduled duration of treatment. In the primary efficacy analysis in confirmatory studies, missing SVR24 data should be considered as non-response.

rules.rule_text: SVR-12 and SVR-24 should be prospectively assessed in phase II and pivotal studies. ETR and time to confirmed undetectable viral load should also be reported.
rules.verbatim_quote: ETR as well as time to confirmed undetectable viral load should be reported. SVR-12 and SVR-24 should be prospectively assessed in phase II and pivotal studies.

rules.rule_text: Pre-enrolment liver biopsy of adequate quality taken no more than 24 months prior to study entry should be used to exclude patients with advanced fibrosis from early clinical trials and to enable stratification by degree of fibrosis in confirmatory studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: For this purpose a liver biopsy of adequate quality, taken no more than 24 months prior to study entry, may be employed. If cirrhosis has been demonstrated, this time window does not apply
rules.exceptions: If non-invasive methods are used as alternative to biopsy, this should be justified by the applicant.

rules.rule_text: Priority DDI studies should cover co-administration with drugs used in management of HCV, HIV, liver transplantation, depression and substance abuse. For nucleoside analogue DAAs, intracellular activation pathway interactions must be investigated.
rules.verbatim_quote: In designing the programme, priority should be given to studies of co-administration with other drugs used in the management of HCV, HIV, liver transplantation, depression and substance abuse... For DAAs that are nucleoside analogues, the potential for drug interactions at the level of intracellular activation by phosphorylation should be considered.

rules.rule_text: An initial application dossier must contain an extensive evaluation of the in-vitro activity of a new DAA, its mechanism of action, activity against viruses other than HCV (HIV, HBV), the risk of selection for drug-resistant variants, and potential for cross-resistance with other agents.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is anticipated that an initial application dossier should contain an extensive evaluation of the in vitro activity of a new DAA, an exploration of its mechanism of action, its activity against viruses other than HCV (HIV, HBV), the risk of selection for drug-resistant variants, and the potential for cross-resistance with other agents.

rules.rule_text: Sequencing and phenotyping studies must be performed on clinical isolates from patients who failed to respond or relapsed. Where a genotypic correlate of resistance has not been observed, phenotypic resistance should also be assessed.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is expected that sequencing and phenotyping studies will be performed on clinical isolates recovered from patients treated with the investigational agent, and that have failed to respond or have relapsed.

rules.rule_text: Clinical efficacy studies are expected to be randomised and, whenever possible, double-blinded. Adherence to therapy must be documented via interview and pill count.
rules.verbatim_quote: Studies are expected to be randomised and, whenever possible, double-blinded. Adherence to therapy is of vital importance for treatment outcome, and major efforts to encourage and document compliance should be undertaken (i.e. interview and pill count).

rules.rule_text: Patients achieving ETR in efficacy studies should be followed for at least 6 months post-treatment to determine SVR. Patients exposed to DAAs not achieving SVR should be monitored for at least one more year with frequent sampling (every 3 months) of HCV-RNA and assessment of genotypic resistance.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients that achieve ETR in efficacy studies should be followed for at least 6 month post treatment. At this time SVR (the primary outcome measure) should be determined. Patients exposed to DAA(s) and not achieving SVR should be monitored for at least one more year after the documentation of nonresponse, with frequent sampling (e.g., every three months) of HCV-RNA and assessment of genotypic resistance.

rules.rule_text: Initial clinical development should enrol subjects with HCV genotype 1 who are naive to HCV therapy or have relapsed after ETR with SOC, do not have advanced fibrosis, and are not co-infected with HIV.
rules.source_section: 4.1 Subject characteristics and selection of subjects
rules.verbatim_quote: It is anticipated that the initial clinical development programme will focus on patients who are infected with HCV genotype 1, are naive to HCV therapy or relapsed after SOC treatment, do not have advanced fibrosis and are not co-infected with HIV.

rules.rule_text: Dose-finding monotherapy studies of 5-7 days duration are acceptable. Monotherapy up to 10-14 days may be acceptable if the compound has a high genetic barrier to resistance (defined as needing >2 mutations to produce IC50 values higher than expected free drug exposure in vivo).
rules.source_section: 4.4.1 Exploratory studies — Dose finding monotherapy studies
rules.verbatim_quote: Currently, 5-(7) days duration of monotherapy is considered acceptable. If there is a strong scientific rationale to prolong this period of monotherapy, and if the compound is foreseen to have a high genetic barrier to resistance, 10-14 days might be acceptable.

rules.rule_text: In early combination studies (DAA + SOC), if treatment-naive and relapsers are included together, stratification by treatment group at randomisation is required. Neither group should constitute less than 40% of the study population.
rules.source_section: 4.4.1 Exploratory studies — Early exploratory studies of combination therapy
rules.verbatim_quote: If both treatment-naive patients and relapsers are included in the same study, they should be stratified by treatment-group at randomisation. It is advised that neither group (naive patients or relapsers) constitute less than 40% of the study population

rules.rule_text: Efficacy against HCV genotype 4 cannot be assumed from efficacy against genotype 1. Patients with genotype 4 should be studied in separate trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: Since efficacy against genotype 4 cannot a priori be assumed for a DAA effective against genotype 1, patients with genotype 4 should be studied in separate trials.

rules.rule_text: For SOC-sparing regimens (DAA combinations without PEG-IFN or ribavirin), dose selection should not be done before early safety and reassuring response (RVR, EVR) data from dose-ranging studies with SOC are available for each of the respective agents.
rules.source_section: 4.4.1 Exploratory studies — Exploratory studies of SOC-sparing regimens
rules.verbatim_quote: It is suggested that dose selection for compounds in the SOC-saving study should not be done before early safety and reassuring response (RVR, EVR) data are available from dose-ranging studies with SOC for each of the respective agents.

rules.rule_text: In confirmatory add-on trials, preliminary stopping rules for insufficient viral response for DAA-containing study arms should have been identified prior to initiation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Prior to the initiation of confirmatory trials, preliminary stopping rules for insufficient viral response for DAA containing study arm(s) should have been identified.

rules.rule_text: If both forms of PEG-IFN (2a and 2b) are used within a confirmatory study, stratification by PEG-IFN type is recommended.
rules.verbatim_quote: If both forms of PEG-IFN (2a and 2b) are used within a study, stratification is recommended.

rules.rule_text: For liver transplant patients with relevant genotype and degree of liver injury, single-arm studies providing PK, safety, and viral response data including SVR may be sufficient to support an indication, provided antiviral activity is of similar magnitude as in non-transplant patients.
rules.verbatim_quote: If a favourable benefit-risk for a DAA containing regimen has been demonstrated in non-transplant patients with relevant genotype and degree of liver injury, single arm studies in transplant patients, designed to provide pharmacokinetics, safety and viral response data, including SVR, may be sufficient to support an indication, provided that the anti-viral activity is of similar magnitude as that seen in non-transplant patients.

rules.rule_text: Safety concerns specific to DAA development for CHC include impaired liver function at baseline, known SOC toxicity, potential for DAA to enhance SOC toxicity, PK interactions, and development of drug resistance. The association between CHC, fatty liver, and insulin resistance has safety monitoring implications.
rules.verbatim_quote: Specific safety concerns related to CHC that are of relevance for the development of new DAAs include impaired liver function at baseline, the known toxicity of current SOC, the potential for SOC toxicity to be enhanced by the DAA, PK interactions and development of drug resistance. In addition, the association between CHC and fatty liver and insulin resistance is of importance, with implications for safety monitoring practises during studies

rules.rule_text: Mechanism-related toxicities (such as mitochondrial toxicity for nucleoside analogues) must be well characterised in non-clinical and clinical studies. Any signals from non-clinical studies must be followed in the clinical development programme.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is expected that mechanism-related toxicities (such as mitochondrial toxicity for nucleoside analogues) will have been well characterised in non-clinical and clinical studies. Any signals that emerge from the non-clinical studies should be followed in the clinical development programme.
